_meta.description: Canonical semantic column ontology with 250+ spreadsheet fields, EN/PT header synonyms, unit hints, and collision-avoidance notes for intelligent column resolution.

fields.collisionNotes: May collide with 'cash_balance' or 'running_balance'. Account balance is a point-in-time snapshot.
See also 'gl_account' for general ledger and 'customer_id' for CRM context.
Current liability. See 'notes_payable' for formal debt instruments.
See also 'trade_receivables' (subset) and 'notes_receivable' (formal instruments).
Revenue earned but not yet invoiced. Opposite of 'deferred_revenue'.
Contra-asset account. Reduces gross fixed assets to net fixed assets.
Subset of 'marketing_expense'. Specifically paid media and ad placements.
For intangible assets. See 'depreciation' for tangible and 'depreciation_and_amortization' for combined.
Inventory valuation method. See 'standard_cost' for predetermined and 'landed_cost' for full cost.
Excludes bonuses, commissions, and benefits. See 'gross_salary' and 'net_salary'.
Manufacturing/inventory batch. See 'lot_number' which is often interchangeable.
Opening cash for the period. See 'ending_cash_balance' for close.
In Brazil, PLR (Participação nos Lucros e Resultados) is common.
Cash outflow for long-term assets. Key component of free cash flow calculation.
Most liquid asset on balance sheet. Includes short-term deposits and money market funds.
Point-in-time cash. See 'cash_and_equivalents' for broader liquid assets.
May collide with 'operating_cash_flow'. Typically interchangeable.
Inverse of 'retention_rate'. Churn + retention should equal 100%.
Generic code field. Prefer more specific variants like 'sku', 'product_id', 'project_code'.

pt: Contribuição para o Financiamento da Seguridade Social

fields.collisionNotes: Brazilian federal tax on gross revenue. Often paired with PIS. See also 'pis'.
Variable pay based on sales. See 'commission_expense' for the company's cost view.
Company's view of commissions paid. See 'commission' for employee's earned amount.
See 'preferred_stock' for the other class of shares.
In Brazil, 'razão social' is the legal name, 'nome fantasia' is the trade name.
Subset of 'professional_fees'. Specifically for consulting engagements.
Revenue minus variable costs. Not the same as 'gross_margin' (which subtracts COGS).
Do not confuse with 'exchange_rate' (currency). Context usually disambiguates.
Direct production costs. See 'cost_of_revenue' for service businesses.
Broader than COGS. Includes service delivery costs for non-manufacturing companies.
What the company paid for an item. See 'unit_price' (sale) and 'landed_cost' (full).
Generic count. See 'quantity' (order context) and 'units' (inventory context).
Accounting credit (right side). Not 'tax_credit'. Context: double-entry bookkeeping.

pt: Contribuição Social sobre o Lucro Líquido

fields.collisionNotes: Brazilian federal tax on net profit. Often paired with IRPJ. See also 'irpj'.
Assets convertible to cash within one year. See 'non_current_assets' for long-term.
Due within one year. See 'non_current_liabilities' for long-term.
Current Assets / Current Liabilities. See 'quick_ratio' for stricter measure.
See also 'nps_score' which measures recommendation likelihood.
Generic date. Prefer specific variants like 'transaction_date', 'invoice_date', etc.
AR / (Revenue/365). Measures collection efficiency.
Accounting debit (left side). Paired with 'credit_amount' in double-entry.
Total Debt / Total Equity. Higher = more leveraged.
Cash received but not yet earned. Opposite of 'accrued_revenue'.
Tax paid in advance. See 'deferred_tax_liability' for the opposite.
Tax owed in the future. See 'deferred_tax_asset' for the opposite.
For tangible/fixed assets. See 'amortization' for intangibles and 'depreciation_and_amortization' for combined.
Combined tangible + intangible write-downs. Key add-back for EBITDA calculation.
Absolute discount amount. See 'discount_percentage' for relative.
Relative discount. Do not confuse with financial 'discount rate' (time value of money).
Net Income / Shares Outstanding. Key investor metric.

pt: lucro antes de juros e impostos

fields.collisionNotes: Revenue - COGS - OpEx. See 'ebitda' (adds back D&A) and 'operating_income' (often synonymous).

en: earnings before interest taxes depreciation amortization
pt: lucro antes de juros impostos depreciação e amortização

fields.collisionNotes: EBIT + D&A. Popular valuation metric. See 'ebit' for after depreciation.
When a contract or policy takes effect. See 'start_date' for projects.
Actual tax paid / pretax income. See 'tax_rate' for statutory rate.
Closing cash for the period. See 'beginning_cash_balance' for opening.
Currency conversion rate. Do not confuse with 'conversion_rate' (sales metric).
Product shelf life or contract expiry. See 'expiry_date' (inventory-specific).
Inventory-specific. See 'expiration_date' for contracts.
Quantity × unit price. Same as 'line_total'.
May collide with 'cash_from_financing'. Typically interchangeable.
May not align with calendar quarter. See 'quarter' for calendar-based.
May not align with calendar year. Common: FY ending Mar 31, Jun 30, Sep 30.
See 'property_plant_equipment' (detailed), 'net_fixed_assets' (after depreciation).
Operating cash flow minus capex. Key valuation metric.
Normalized headcount. 2 half-time employees = 1.0 FTE. See 'headcount' for raw count.
Often combined with selling as SG&A. See 'selling_expenses'.
Accounting-specific. See 'account_number' for banking and 'cost_center' for management.

pt: ágio por expectativa de rentabilidade futura

fields.collisionNotes: Premium paid in acquisitions above fair value. Subject to impairment testing.
(Revenue - COGS) / Revenue. See 'gross_profit' for the absolute amount.
Revenue minus COGS. See 'gross_margin' for percentage form.
Before deductions (returns, discounts). See 'net_revenue' for after deductions.
Before deductions (tax, benefits). See 'net_salary' for take-home.
Raw count of employees. See 'full_time_equivalent' for normalized FTE measure.
May collide with 'start_date'. Hire date is HR-specific; start date is project-specific.

pt: Imposto sobre Circulação de Mercadorias e Serviços

fields.collisionNotes: Brazilian state-level tax on goods and services. Rates vary by state (7-25%). See also 'vat'.
Generic identifier. Prefer specific variants like 'customer_id', 'transaction_id', etc.
Generic. In Brazil, see 'irpj' (corporate) or IRPF (personal).
Includes patents, trademarks, software. See 'goodwill' (separate intangible).
Cost of debt. See 'interest_income' for the asset side.
Income from deposits and investments. See 'interest_expense' for the cost side.
Can be for lending or borrowing. Context determines if lower or higher is better.
COGS / Average Inventory. Higher = faster-moving inventory.
Monetary value of inventory. See 'stock_quantity' for unit count.
May collide with 'cash_from_investing'. Typically interchangeable.
Brazilian federal excise tax on manufactured products. Rate varies by product.
Brazilian corporate income tax. Standard rate 15% + 10% surcharge. See also 'csll'.

pt: Imposto sobre Serviços de Qualquer Natureza

fields.collisionNotes: Brazilian municipal tax on services. Rates vary by municipality (2-5%).
Purchase price + freight + duties + insurance. See 'cost_price' for base and 'average_cost' for weighted.
Subset of 'professional_fees'. Specifically for legal services.
Quantity × unit price for a single line item. Same concept as 'extended_price'.
Published/catalog price before discounts. See 'selling_price' for actual transaction price.
Debt maturing beyond one year. See 'short_term_debt' for current portion.
Investments held beyond one year. See 'short_term_investments' for current.
Often interchangeable with 'batch_number'. Lot is common in pharma/food.
Broader than 'advertising_expense'. Includes events, content, brand, etc.
Absolute markup amount. See 'markup_percentage' for relative.
(Selling Price - Cost) / Cost. Not the same as margin % which uses selling price as base.
Sum of operating + investing + financing cash flows.
Fixed assets minus accumulated depreciation. See 'fixed_assets' for gross.
After all expenses and taxes. Often confused with 'net_profit' (interchangeable) or 'net_revenue' (different concept).
Net Income / Revenue. See 'gross_margin' and 'operating_margin' for intermediate margins.
Interchangeable with 'net_income' in most contexts.
After returns, discounts, allowances. See 'gross_revenue' for before deductions.
After deductions (tax, benefits). See 'gross_salary' for before deductions.
Often interchangeable with 'net_revenue'. Some companies use 'sales' for units and 'revenue' for currency.
Assets not expected to convert to cash within one year. See 'current_assets'.
Due beyond one year. See 'current_liabilities' for short-term.
One-time items. See 'recurring_revenue' for the ongoing component.
Formal written promises to pay. See 'accounts_payable' for trade payables.
Formal written promises to receive. See 'accounts_receivable' for trade.
Range: -100 to +100. See 'customer_satisfaction' for CSAT metric.
Physical count only. See 'stock_quantity' which may include allocated/reserved.
May collide with 'cash_from_operations'. Typically interchangeable.
Excludes COGS. Includes SG&A, R&D, D&A. See 'total_expenses' for all-inclusive.
Revenue - COGS - OpEx. Often synonymous with 'ebit'.
Operating Income / Revenue. Between gross margin and net margin.
Income outside core operations. See 'interest_income', 'dividend_income' for specific types.
Outflow. See 'transaction_amount' (generic) and 'total_amount' (invoice total).
Employer-side taxes. In Brazil, includes INSS patronal, FGTS, etc.
Informal count unit. See 'units' (formal) and 'quantity' (order context).
Brazilian federal contribution on revenue. Often paired with COFINS. See also 'cofins'.
See 'common_stock' for the other share class.
After interest, before income tax. Same as 'profit_before_tax'.
See also 'sku' which is warehouse/retail-specific.
Revenue from goods. See 'service_revenue' for services.
Umbrella for 'consulting_fees' and 'legal_fees'.
Same as 'net_income' and 'net_profit'.
Same as 'pretax_income'.
Detailed view. See 'fixed_assets' (often synonymous) and 'net_fixed_assets' (after depreciation).
In Brazil, IPTU (urban) and ITR (rural). See also 'excise_tax'.
Most common count field in orders/invoices. See 'units' and 'count' for alternatives.
Calendar quarter. See 'fiscal_quarter' for fiscal year alignment.
(Current Assets - Inventory) / Current Liabilities. Stricter than 'current_ratio'.
Revenue expected to continue. See 'subscription_revenue' (subset) and 'non_recurring_revenue'.
R&D may be capitalized or expensed depending on accounting standards.
Inverse of 'churn_rate'. Retention + churn should sum to 100%.
Net Income / Total Assets. Measures asset efficiency.
Net Income / Shareholders Equity. Key investor metric.
Generic return metric. More specific than ROA or ROE.
Often confused with 'net_revenue' or 'gross_revenue'. Prefer the more specific variant when both exist.
Progressive ledger balance. Not the same as 'account_balance' (snapshot).
Generic term. See 'base_salary', 'gross_salary', 'net_salary' for specific variants.
Often interchangeable with 'revenue'. Some companies distinguish: sales = units, revenue = currency.
Generic term. In Brazil, see 'icms' for state-level equivalent.
Often combined with G&A as SG&A. See 'general_and_administrative'.
Actual transaction price. May differ from 'list_price' due to discounts.
Revenue from services. See 'product_revenue' for goods.
Total Assets minus Total Liabilities. Synonym of 'total_equity'.
Debt maturing within one year. See 'long_term_debt' for non-current.
Investments maturing within one year. See 'long_term_investments'.
See also 'product_id'. SKU is typically warehouse/retail-specific.
Predetermined cost for variance analysis. See 'average_cost' for actual weighted average.
Project-specific. May collide with 'effective_date' (contracts) or 'hire_date' (HR).
May include allocated/reserved. See 'on_hand_quantity' for physical count.
Subset of 'recurring_revenue'. Specifically subscription-based.
Sum before tax. See 'total_amount' for the final figure.
Reduces tax owed. Not the same as 'credit_amount' (accounting debit/credit).
Accrued tax expense on P&L. Not the same as 'tax_payable' (balance sheet).
Statutory/nominal rate. See 'effective_tax_rate' for actual blended rate.
Final amount including all taxes. See 'subtotal' for pre-tax.
Must equal total liabilities + total equity.
Short-term + long-term debt. Not the same as 'total_liabilities' (includes non-debt items).
Often interchangeable with 'shareholders_equity'.
May collide with 'total_revenue'. Total income can include non-operating items.
Not the same as 'total_debt' (financial obligations only).
Use when multiple revenue streams are summed. See 'revenue' for generic.
Subset of 'accounts_receivable'. Specifically from trade/customer transactions.
Generic. See 'payment_amount' (outflow) and 'total_amount' (invoice total).
Contra-equity account. Shares bought back by the company.
Price per single unit. See 'list_price' (catalog) and 'selling_price' (transaction).
Standard count. See 'pieces' (informal) and 'quantity' (generic).
International term. In Brazil, ICMS serves a similar role at state level.
Tax deducted at source. In Brazil, IRRF is the most common.
Current Assets minus Current Liabilities. See 'current_ratio' for ratio form.
